DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
accessibility artifact-reference example extensions guideline link metadata rich-text status usecase
anatomy api component design-specifications state variant
token
style
pattern

DSDS Style Definitions

Definitions for documenting macro-level visual style guidelines — color palettes, typography systems, spacing scales, elevation levels, and other visual attributes. Style documentation provides the rationale, principles, and usage rules that govern how token values are applied across the system.

Source: style/style.schema.json

4 definitions in this file:

  • principle
  • scaleStep
  • scale
  • styleDoc

principle

A high-level guiding principle for the visual style.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
titlestringrequiredA short name for the principle.
descriptionrichTextrequired

References: richText

scaleStep

A single step in an ordered scale (e.g., a type size, a spacing value, an elevation level).

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
tokenstringrequiredThe token name for this step. MUST reference a token defined in the token schema.
labelstringoptionalA human-readable label (e.g., 'sm', 'md', 'lg', '2xl').
valuestringoptionalThe resolved value for display (e.g., '16px', '#1a1a1a', '400').
descriptionrichTextoptional

References: richText

scale

An ordered sequence of values that forms a visual scale (e.g., type scale, spacing scale, elevation scale). Each step references a design token.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable name of the scale.
descriptionrichTextrequired
stepsscaleStep[]requiredThe ordered steps in the scale, from smallest/lowest to largest/highest.
Min items: 1
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name.

References: richText, scaleStep

styleDoc

Documentation for a macro-level visual style — the principles, guidelines, scales, and token references that govern a visual attribute such as color, typography, spacing, or elevation across the entire system.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable identifier (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation').
Pattern: ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$
displayNamestringrequiredHuman-readable name.
descriptionrichTextrequired
statusstatusrequired
categorystringrequiredThe visual attribute category (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation', 'motion', 'shape').
summarystringoptionalA one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup.
deprecationNoticestringoptionalRequired when status is 'deprecated'.
sincestringoptionalThe version in which this style was introduced.
tagsstring[]optionalFreeform keywords to relate guidelines across categories/disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation').
principlesprinciple[]optionalHigh-level guiding principles for this visual style.
tokenGroupsartifactReference[]optionalReferences to the token groups that implement this style. Connects macro-level guidance to concrete token values defined in the token schema.
scalesscale[]optionalOrdered scales that define the progression of values for this style (e.g., a type scale, a spacing scale, an elevation scale). Each scale step references a design token.
useCasesuseCasesoptional
guidelinesguideline[]optionalUsage guidelines for the style. Each entry is a self-contained guideline. Use the `category` property on individual guidelines to group by concern (e.g., 'design', 'accessibility', 'content').
accessibilityaccessibilityObjectoptional
linkslink[]optionalExternal resource links and related artifact references.
$extensionsextensionsoptional

Conditional: When status is "deprecated", then deprecationNotice is required.

References: richText, status, principle, artifactReference, scale, useCases, guideline, accessibilityObject, link, extensions
